title: Fix S3 Vectors auto-configuration fallback for Vector Topic Memory
description:
Observed runtime error in recent report runs:

`Vector Topic Memory: S3 Vectors not configured — set s3_vectors.bucket_name and s3_vectors.index_name.`

Goal:
- make Semantic Reinforcement Memory resolve S3 vector bucket/index defaults automatically for the prototype stack when explicit config is absent
- keep explicit `s3_vectors.*` config/env override behavior intact
- verify with targeted tests and a local run path check

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Starting fix: updating VectorTopicMemory config resolution so S3 vectors bucket/index default from prototype naming convention when explicit config/env is absent.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Root cause identified: report templates provide s3_vectors.region only, while block currently requires explicit bucket/index. Implementing fallback to prototype naming convention based on ENVIRONMENT with explicit config/env taking precedence.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Code patch complete: VectorTopicMemory now resolves default S3 vector bucket/index from ENVIRONMENT when explicit settings are absent; tests updated for new behavior. Running targeted pytest now.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Validation: pytest targeted suite passes (9 passed). Runtime-path sanity check confirms defaults resolve to bucket=plexus-vectors-development and index=topic-memory-idx-development when explicit settings are absent.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Fix delivered in commit 1a4126b8 (pushed to origin/feature/reinforcement-memory): VectorTopicMemory now auto-resolves S3 vector bucket/index defaults from ENVIRONMENT for prototype runs, while preserving explicit overrides.
